w:r.w:t: This is where the math meets the grit.
Until now, we have built the physical universe, the characters, the enemies, and the items. But without
, those entities just stand there staring at each other.
of your game. They do not store character sheets; they take two JSON objects, smash them together using your exact dice rules, and spit out the bloody result.
Here are the developer-ready blueprints for
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 06: The Skill Check Manager (The Fate Engine)

A stateless Python microservice. It is responsible for resolving all non-combat friction. It handles the 36 Tactical Triads, calculates Advantage/Disadvantage, rolls the virtual dice, and strictly enforces the
progression system by telling the GM App when to award a Pip.
│ ├── schemas.py # Pydantic definitions for the 36 Triads
The VTT sends the exact stat pairs (e.g., Knowledge + Logic) and the engine does the rest.
# Players can burn Focus to add dice/bonuses
# GM toggles this to allow "Survivor" pips
# Needed to check for Nat 1s and Nat 20s
This strictly enforces the rules from your
When the VTT hits POST /api/skills/roll, this happens:
dice_roller.py rolls 1d20 (rolling twice if Advantage/Disadvantage is true).
It adds the lead_stat_value, trail_stat_value, and skill_rank.
It checks pip_calculator.py using the raw die face.
It returns the final JSON to the GM App.
(The GM App then automatically ticks the Pip box on the Character Sheet if a trigger was returned).
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 07: The Rules Manager (The Clash Engine)

A stateless Python microservice. This is the crown jewel of your tactical combat. It completely replaces the boring "Armor Class" system of D&D. It takes an Attacker and a Defender, rolls for both, compares the math, and outputs the exact state change (Crushing Win, Scrape, Reversal, or Deadlock).
│ └── injury_applier.py # Converts damage into the Dual-Track Injury slots
Notice how we feed the engine everything: Stats, Weapons, and the Stamina the player decided to "burn" to push their luck.
# Higher chaos = deadlocks cause wild environmental shifts
This is the pure mathematical translation of your Contested Combat design doc.
# 1. Roll the dice for both sides and add their burned Stamina/Focus
# 2. Determine Outcome based on the Margin
# Roll full weapon damage + trigger chaos/tactical effect
# Defender wins the clash! They get a free counter-attack or chip damage.
# Both parties are locked. If chaos is high, trigger a world event.
"Reality Warp: Ground shifts, both fall Prone."
If a character drops to 0 HP during the Clash, this script intercepts the result and translates it into your Dual-Track system.
If defender_hp_change reduces HP to 0 -> set defender_injury_applied = "1 Major Body Injury (Shattered Bone)".
When the dice stop rolling, the VTT receives this mathematically bulletproof JSON. The VTT draws the blood spatters, updates the HP bars, and hands this to the AI Narrator.
"Attacker rolled 18 (14 + 4 Stamina Burn). Defender rolled 15. Margin: +3."
"Kaelen won by a Scrape margin. Defender takes 4 damage. Kaelen is exhausted (Lost 4 Stamina)."
Why this is the ultimate tactical engine:
By turning the combat rules into a standalone API:
Your React frontend never has to calculate math. It just sends {"attacker_stamina_burned": 4} and waits to see who bleeds.

Your AI DM (The Narrator agent) doesn't have to guess if an attack hit. It reads the "clash_result": "SCRAPE" and natively knows to narrate a gritty, desperate, glancing blow instead of a clean decapitation.
If so, we only have one batch left.
(the central hub that routes all this JSON) and the
(which strings the nodes and encounters together). Ready to finish the factory line?
